summary: Recency sweep (Nov 2025 - Apr 2026): 0 new concurrent work found. SRA (AAAI 2026) and SMRA (arXiv Jan 2026) remain the only papers in the supervised-attention-for-HSD cluster. Neither uses sparsemax. The Method x Task gap is confirmed as of 2026-04-07. Gate N1 PROCEED decision stands.
critical_context: Primary competitor: eilertsen2025aligning (SRA, AAAI 2026) — softmax + KL loss on HateXplain
Secondary: vargas2026smra (SMRA, Jan 2026) — MFT moral rationales, Portuguese, softmax
Both from Vargas research group — potential future follow-up paper is a competitive risk
Sparsemax x human rationale supervision x HSD gap: CONFIRMED UNOCCUPIED
Proceed to design-experiments with N1 routing constraints (H1, H4, H2 must-have; H3, H5, W5a should-have)
